title: Kill Bill
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
I'm still a fan even though I was salty
  > [#1, 90 votes] SZA likens herself to Bill from the 2003-2004 Kill Bill film duology. Bill crashes and massacres the wedding of his ex-lover Beatrix Kiddo after finding out she faked her death to get away from him....
Hate to see you with some other broad, know you happy
Hate to see you happy if I'm not the one drivin'

[Pre-Chorus]
I'm so mature, I'm so mature
  > [#2, 81 votes] In Kill Bill: Vol 2., protagonist Beatrix Kiddo is praised by Bill for her maturity. When telling her about the legend of Pai Mei, Bill notably says: One of the things I’ve always liked about you,...
I'm so mature, I got me a therapist to tell me there's other men
  > [#3, 41 votes] In SZA’s 2017 hit single, “Drew Barrymore,” she mentions the idea of going to therapy to overcome her self esteem. I get so lonely, I forget what I’m worth We get so lonely, we pretend that this...
I don't want none, I just want you
If I can't have you, no one should

[Chorus]
I might
  > [#4, 116 votes] On the surface, SZA sings about wanting to kill her ex and his new girlfriend. However, this notably coincides with the plot of the iconic 2003-2004 Kill Bill film duology. SZA likens herself to...
I might kill my ex, not the best idea
His new girlfriend's next, how'd I get here?
I might kill my ex, I still love him though
Rather be in jail than alone
  > [#5, 79 votes] SZA’s never been comfortable by herself. She’s always sought to have others by her side, primarily for validation because of her insecurities, and was always left even more insecure after being...

[Verse 2]
I get the sense that it's a lost cause
I get the sense that you might really love her
This text gon' be evidence, this text is evidence
I tried to ration with you, no murders or crime of passion, but damn
You was out of reach
You was at the farmer's market with your perfect peach
  > [#6, 44 votes] Peach emoji in texts is often referring to butt in a sexual manner. Sometimes women are profanely called “a piece of ass” which is widely popular in rap and hip-hop culture. Here SZA uses a play on...
Now I'm in the basement plannin' home invasion
Now you layin' face-down, got me singin' over a beat

[Pre-Chorus]
I'm so mature, I'm so mature
  > [#2, see above]
I'm so mature, I got me a therapist to tell me there's other men
  > [#3, see above]
I don't want none, I just want you
If I can't have you, no one will

[Chorus]
I might (I)
  > [#4, see above]
I might kill my ex, not the best idea
His new girlfriend's next, how'd I get here?
I might kill my ex, I still love him though
Rather be in jail than alone
  > [#5, see above]

[Bridge]
I did it all for love (Love)
  > [#7, 70 votes] SZA acknowledges that her intentions are homicidal, premeditated, and bafflingly driven by a deep love and devotion. She also seems to be admitting to first-degree murder without hesitation, while...
I did it all on no drugs (Drugs)
I did all of this sober
I did it all for us, oh
I did it all for love (Love)
I did all of this on no drugs (Drugs)
I did all of this sober
Don't you know I did it all for us? (I'm gon' kill your ass tonight)

[Chorus]
Oh, I just killed my ex (My ex, oops), not the best idea (Idea)
  > [#4, see above]
Killed his girlfriend next, how'd I get here? (He left me no choice)
I just killed my ex (My ex), I still love him though (I do)
Rather be in Hell than alone
  > [#5, see above]
